Tackling the impossible, together

In 2017, Covariant's founding researchers set out to take their advancements from a lab to the real settings in need of AI Robotics.

Seven years later, our growing Covariant team around the world continues to set a new standard for what’s possible in AI and the industries it’s applied to. We’re building robots with the ability to see, think, and act – and improving how the world works – every day.

## Our journey

Covariant founded

Covariant raises $7M in seed funding

* * *

2017

In the beginning…

Pieter Abbeel, Peter Chen, Rocky Duan, and Tianhao Zhang team up to find ways to apply their cutting-edge AI research to solve real-world problems.

Covariant founded

Covariant raises $7M in seed funding

The Covariant Brain reaches human-level autonomy

* * *

2018

The building years

The team creates the Covariant Brain and the first AI Robotic solutions to automate pick-and-place processes at warehouses and fulfillment centers.

The Covariant Brain reaches human-level autonomy

Total funding increases to $27M

Covariant wins ABB AI Robotics competition

Covariant Robotic Goods-to-Person Picking released

* * *

2019

Proving AI Robotics in the real world

Leading warehouse integrators (ABB, KNAPP, Bastian) and customers worldwide select the Covariant Brain as their AI Robotics platform to automate robotic picking.

Total funding increases to $27M

Covariant wins ABB AI Robotics competition

Covariant Robotic Goods-to-Person Picking released

First pharmaceutical customer

First industrial goods customer

Total funding further increases to $67M

Covariant Robotic Putwall released

* * *

2020

Expanding offerings

Covariant continues to develop and deploy new solutions to address different pick-and-place use cases in warehouses.

First pharmaceutical customer

First industrial goods customer

Total funding further increases to $67M

Covariant Robotic Putwall released

First health & beauty customer

Covariant reaches $147M in total funding

Covariant Robotic Induction released

First apparel customer

* * *

2021

Rising market confidence and demand

Driven by the real-world performance of AI Robotics, customer confidence and demand increases for intelligent warehouse automation.

First health & beauty customer

Covariant reaches $147M in total funding

Covariant Robotic Induction released

First apparel customer

London office opened to serve European customers

Covariant Robotic Depalletization released

First home improvement customer

Covariant Robotic Kitting released

* * *

2022

Scaling up

Covariant customers and partners continue to scale the number of robotic stations they have deployed for multiple use cases across multiple warehouses.

London office opened to serve European customers

Covariant Robotic Depalletization released

First home improvement customer

Covariant Robotic Kitting released

First grocery customer

Total funding grows to $222M

* * *

2023

Continued growth

Ecommerce trends and labor market dynamics drive growing demand and investment in AI Robotics for warehouse automation.

First grocery customer

Total funding grows to $222M

Covariant launches RFM-1, the first commercial Robotics Foundation Model

* * *

2024

Pushing the frontier again

AI Robotics reaches another inflection point with robots delivering unprecedented flexibility and reliability powered by Robotics Foundation Models.

Covariant launches RFM-1, the first commercial Robotics Foundation Model

* * *

2025

Backed by visionary investors
